meta.title: BMW M70 / M73 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW M70 / M73 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW M70 and M73 are BMW's flagship V12 petrol engines — smooth and powerful, but complex and expensive to maintain. Backed by real UK specialist enquiries [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 85% confidence — based on specialist workshop consensus and owner‑club documented history.

rows.score: 12 (V12 complexity adds labour time; some repairs are manageable, others require engine-out)
12 (Some parts still available but expensive; many items NLA or specialist‑only)
16 (Reconditioned cost vs. lowest host‑value (~£5,000) = ~150% – over threshold but classic values support → 16)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M70 and M73 are refined, durable V12s — but complexity and parts scarcity make ownership costly — buy only with full documented history.
verdictRating.bestFor: Collectors and enthusiasts who value V12 smoothness and are prepared for specialist maintenance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're on a tight budget or cannot afford V12‑level parts and labour costs.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M70/M73 typically takes 20–28 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent specialist) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE prestige specialist) [BMW‑QUOTE].

cta.label: → Compare BMW M70/M73 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does a BMW M70/M73 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M70 or M73 typically costs £6,000–£9,000 supply only [BMW‑QUOTE], varying by exact variant and condition. Fitting adds £2,000–£4,500 depending on specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Is the BMW M70/M73 a reliable engine?
items.answer: Yes – the M70 and M73 are fundamentally robust V12s with forged steel crankshafts and durable bottom ends. However, age‑related issues (cooling system, ignition components, oil leaks) are common and expensive. Regular maintenance is essential.

items.question: What is the most common BMW M70/M73 failure?
items.answer: Cooling system failure is the most critical – plastic radiator necks and expansion tanks degrade with age, causing overheating and potential head gasket failure. Also common are ignition coil failure (12 coils for V12, expensive), intake manifold gasket leaks, and oil leaks from valve covers.

items.question: Which BMW models use the M70/M73?
items.answer: The M70 powered the E31 850i, E32 750iL, and the rare E34 550i prototype. The M73 appeared in the E31 850Ci (1994–1999) and E38 750iL (1994–2001).

items.question: How long does the BMW M70/M73 last?
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance, these engines can exceed 200,000 miles. However, the cost of a full rebuild (£9,000+) and parts scarcity make preventative maintenance financially sensible.
